key_insight: Cayley graphs provide a learnable geometric proxy for finite-group structure, allowing algebraic properties to be inferred from graph observables and models.
engineering_relevance: The work offers a large, labeled benchmark for graph-based representation learning and feature engineering on structured algebraic data.
method: Built a dataset of 131,406 Cayley graphs for all groups of order ≤ 767 except 512.
Computed exact group-property labels plus graph, cycle, distance, and spectral statistics.
Compared classical baselines, an MLP, and GNNs (notably GIN and some GCN settings) for property prediction.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports broad empirical evaluation across engineered statistics and neural models, with strong signal from graph features and substantial predictive performance from GNNs.
watch_item: Results may depend on fixed-order settings and handcrafted graph statistics, so generalization to unseen group orders or different graph constructions should be tested carefully.
paper_title: Learning the Graphical Nature of Symmetries
